@avafe hackernews Open Game Development At some point in the video game life cycle — especially in live service games — they reach a sort of “maintenance” stage. In this stage, the game still benefits from content updates, but the core gameplay is more or less complete.

This stage of development is tedious, and takes away from the time developers could spend creating new games. Despite developer
